hook: 🚀 Today, we're announcing the public launch of Erdo, the AI Workforce for Business (full post in comments).
text: 🚀 Today, we're announcing the public launch of Erdo, the AI Workforce for Business (full post in comments). We're not far from a world where every employee, executive or founder has dozens of AI agents actively doing work for them in a way that is highly proactive, understands the bigger picture, and action-oriented. Making that vision a reality is exactly what has driven me and my co-founders Emi Gal (Non-exec Chairman) and Niall Brickell (CTO) to build erdo.ai. We're automating the insight-to-action loop across hundreds of use cases: 📈 For marketing teams: • Sales dip detected → Google/Meta budgets auto-reallocated. No human intervention needed. • Full technical SEO audit done in minutes, not weeks. • Competitors' ads and influencer activity monitored in real-time. 💼 For B2B sales: • Deal goes stale → personalised re-engagement drafted and sent. • CRM updated automatically by your RevOps agent. • Post-sales reporting delivered without lifting a finger. 🛒 For e-commerce: • Shopify revenue drops below threshold → CRM campaign targeting top customers fires instantly. 📅 For everyone: • 24/7 secure monitoring of your key business metrics to surface insights & opportunities • Calendar managed. Emails summarized. Briefings prepared. Done. & much more No 9-month deployment cycle. No $20M budget. ✅ Minutes to set up. ✅ Built for operators and doers. ✅ Works where you already work, including Slack. 👉 Try it today: erdo.ai
title: 🚀 Today, we're announcing the public launch of Erdo, the AI Workforce for Business (full post in comments).

hook: We are in the manifesto porn era of AI.
text: We are in the manifesto porn era of AI. I think I'm here for it. Seems like every week there's a new 20-page prophecy delivered in the same Sarah Connor-style fluent apocal-ish. What may have started with decrees from the high priests of foundation model companies has spilled over to the ranks of hedge fund quants and engineering middle management. I thought AI would kill the art of articulation, but it turns out maybe it bestows all of us with creative writing degrees and minors in Slack channel stimulation. Today, I discovered this fascinating one from Geoff McDonald, tl;dr — malicious AI worms will completely reimagine threats to cybersecurity. Something for everybody here, but my takeaway: we have a lot of work to do in consumer security right now...here's why: + 25 years ago, the cybersecurity market was <$10B, split roughly 60% enterprise and 40% consumer. + Today, the market is ~$250B, split 90% enterprise and 10% consumer, and enterprise cybersecurity startups capture >95% of new venture capital. There used to be a time when consumer security companies led the way. Firms like McAfee and Norton pioneered threat research and antivirus innovation. Now, that work is done for large enterprises, while consumers wait longer and hope it trickles down to improve their protection. Somewhere along the way, we lost some innovation muscle in protecting the digital daily lives of everyday people and families. We started Savi Security because we need to get busy building it back.
title: We are in the manifesto porn era of AI.

hook: We just announced our $60M series C funding led by Insight Partners, bringing our total fundraise till date to $105M.
text: We just announced our $60M series C funding led by Insight Partners, bringing our total fundraise till date to $105M. We’ve gotten to know the team at Insight Partners, Apoorva Goyal Nagu Chidambaram Kedar Venkatesh Samma Hafeez Santosh Iyer Jeffrey Lieberman, and others, over the last few years. They are company builders and true partners and I cannot be more excited about bringing them on as our latest investors. When we started the company 6 yrs ago, we didn’t anticipate how AI would change the world, and nor did we anticipate how central professional services teams would be to Enterprise AI transformation. The teams deploying AI are now the difference between software that turns into shelfware, and software that delivers outcomes. These teams now HAVE to scale their impact to more customers, existing and new, every year. They need to transform and re-invent how they work. This is the biggest opportunity for services teams and leaders to show up as the hero of the story. The spotlight is on them already - it’s all about Services-Led Growth in the “Outcome Era” of enterprise AI. So at Rocketlane, we’re going all-in on being the partner on this transformation for services teams. Our AI platform Nitro will give these services teams super powers to re-invent how they work - migration and data transformation agents, configuration agents, documentation agents that teams can start delegating work to. “Cursor for PS teams”, if you will. We’re thankful to our early investors Z47 Nexus Venture Partners 8VC and all our angels too 🙂. Your belief and partnership set us on this path. We’re also thankful to all our customers for your trust, partnership, and feedback - we shaped this together, and we now have more firepower for the next phase of execution. PSA was built to track work. We’ve now also built the platform that also executes the work. If you want to see what Nitro can do for your team, just comment on this post, happy to set something up. Cc Deepak Bala Vignesh Girishankar Vikram Vaidyanathan Jishnu B. Jack Moshkovich Pranay Desai Arjun Gandhi
title: We just announced our $60M series C funding led by Insight Partners, bringing our total fundraise till date to $105M.

hook: Over the past two months I've interviewed more than a dozen founders from breakout AI-native companies.
text: Over the past two months I've interviewed more than a dozen founders from breakout AI-native companies. What they do differently ⤵️ Some of the companies already scaled to $100M ARR and beyond – including Clay, Gamma, HeyGen, Intercom (Fin.ai), and Fireworks AI. Others are fast-growing AI-native startups who are well-positioned to join them including 7AI, AirOps, bolt.new, Fyxer, GC AI, GrowthX AI, HappyRobot, and incident.io. Read the full post in today's Growth Unhinged: https://lnkd.in/eMGzZRbx Here’s a preview of some of the more interesting takeaways: 1. Finding PMF in SaaS was an artisanal endeavor; it was usually careful, steady, gradual, and incremental. For AI-native startups PMF was often obvious and immediate. 2. AI-native companies reached $1 million ARR twice as fast as B2B SaaS. It took the average company I interviewed only 12 months (!) from when they started building & 7 months from having a usable live product. 3. Price sensitivity drops dramatically when AI does real work. Gamma A/B tested pricing and found that people were willing to pay double the price of a conventional productivity app. The highest price they tested ($20 per month) performed best. 4. Product success hinges on AI performance crossing a reliability threshold. 5. Explicit user feedback (thumbs up/down) can be surprisingly insightful on its own. 6. SaaS companies usually hired their first AEs as they approached $1 million ARR. AI companies tend to delay hiring their first AE until about $2-5M ARR. GrowthX AI reached $8M ARR purely through founder-led sales. 6. The winning GTM motions combine bottom-up discovery with top-down sales. Interestingly, half of the AI-native companies I interviewed started with a primarily self-serve GTM. Only one is still primarily focused on self-serve today. 7. The best GTM teams look more like consulting firms than SaaS sellers. Their sales teams aren’t selling features; they’re selling a business transformation powered by AI. --- 🙏 HUGE thank you to those who participated: Varun Anand (Clay), Jon Noronha (Gamma), Joshua X. (HeyGen), Des Traynor (Intercom, Fin.ai), Lin Qiao (Fireworks AI), Archie Hollingsworth (Fyxer), Lior Div (7AI), Matt Hammel (AirOps), Alexander Berger (bolt.new), Cecilia Ziniti (GC AI), Marcel Santilli (GrowthX), Pablo Palafox (HappyRobot), and Stephen Whitworth (incident.io). --- Today's newsletter is supported by AirOps who just released a fantastic new AI search playbook for 2026. I'll drop the link in the comments.
title: Over the past two months I've interviewed more than a dozen founders from breakout AI-native companies.

hook: Today I am launching a search for what I believe is the most exciting job in performance marketing in the country: Executive Vice President, Performance Marketing at Rocket.
text: Today I am launching a search for what I believe is the most exciting job in performance marketing in the country: Executive Vice President, Performance Marketing at Rocket. Rocket invests significantly in performance marketing, driving quality leads across Redfin and Rocket. Redfin is the country’s most engaging home search platform. Rocket Mortgage is the country’s biggest mortgage originator, now responsible for 1 in every 6 mortgages in America. Together they form the world’s first fully integrated homeownership platform. Our brand momentum is equally powerful. In just 14 months, we’ve grown unaided awareness from 18% to 41% amongst our priority growth audiences. Our recent Super Bowl campaign featuring Lady Gaga and “Won’t You Be My Neighbor,” alongside Redfin’s “Great American Home Search,” generated the highest media impact of any advertiser, with an estimated $195M in value. This is the wind at the back of an already world-class performance marketing engine. This executive will lead a team of nearly 100 people across every performance marketing discipline. They will be a proven, high-profile leader who has operated at scale, manage a significant part of Rocket's +$1B marketing budget, and who thrives in dynamic complexity with a clear, agile vision. The role reports directly to me and sits on Rocket’s Chief Executive Leadership Team. That means this person will be one of 20 executives stewarding the strategic direction of the entire enterprise. I am only looking for the best. If that is you, or you know who that is, please apply through the link in the comments. That is the only channel of communication for this search. Community, please share with your network. Thank you.
title: Today I am launching a search for what I believe is the most exciting job in performance marketing in the country: Executive Vice President, Performance Marketing at Rocket.

hook: Spent the day at Y Combinator's W26 Demo Day in San Francisco, and a few things stood out from Garry Tan’s opening remarks: • ~90% of the batch is AI • Solo founders can now build faster than entire teams could just a few years ago • Garry said he's built more software this year than in all of 2013 when he founded Posthaven and expects 20× that output over the rest of 2026 • Companies in the batch are averaging ~14% weekly revenue growth • 14 companies are already at $1M+ ARR • 23,000 applications were received, with <1% accepted • ~70% of the batch applied with zero revenue My big takeaway: AI isn’t just another category in this batch; it’s foundational for building today.
text: Spent the day at Y Combinator's W26 Demo Day in San Francisco, and a few things stood out from Garry Tan’s opening remarks: • ~90% of the batch is AI • Solo founders can now build faster than entire teams could just a few years ago • Garry said he's built more software this year than in all of 2013 when he founded Posthaven and expects 20× that output over the rest of 2026 • Companies in the batch are averaging ~14% weekly revenue growth • 14 companies are already at $1M+ ARR • 23,000 applications were received, with <1% accepted • ~70% of the batch applied with zero revenue My big takeaway: AI isn’t just another category in this batch; it’s foundational for building today. Also, fun seeing prolific YC startup investor Joe Montana join one company’s pitch on stage. And great to see so many fellow Pioneer Fund investors, including Russell Smith and Taro Fukuyama, as well. The pace founders can move at right now is mind blowing, and the future is bright.

hook: Last week I hit 25k followers… so thought it’s time to reintroduce myself for everyone new.
text: Last week I hit 25k followers… so thought it’s time to reintroduce myself for everyone new. I started writing on here with zero expectations It’s turned into one of my fav things to do A bit about myself in 3 phases: Phase I -Was a YouTuber in 2008 filming about tech/ the App Store -First marketing job at 18 years old at software company -Graduated UC Berkeley where I met best friends (oh, and learned a thing or two) Phase II -Started my tech career at Postmates (gosh what fun times during the delivery wars) -Met my now-fiancé while living in San Francisco (highlight of all) -Joined Uber and learned quick that corporate wasn’t for me -Started writing for TechCrunch on growth marketing -Left Uber to join Coinbase— needed to scratch my crypto itch Phase III -Founder full-time (14 year old me building random wordpress startups would be proud) -My first global staffing company acquired after reaching ~$4m arr -Wrote a book about scaling from 0 —> 1 available on Amazon -Co-founded GrowthPair (second global staffing co) with Alex Lieberman to bring top 1% global marketers to companies -Launched Claude Marketers to teach Claude Cowork/ Code to the next generation of marketers Phase IIII (in progress) -Scaling GrowthPair while sharing AI learnings on here -Wedding later this year in San Diego -Followed by honeymoon in Australia/ New Zealand Welcome new followers. Pumped to continue sharing about AI marketing which eats up most of my brainpower these days
title: Last week I hit 25k followers… so thought it’s time to reintroduce myself for everyone new.

hook: 10 years ago, Eilon and I had this wild idea: What if you could capture EVERY customer interaction, understand it with AI, and actually run your revenue org off of it?
text: 10 years ago, Eilon and I had this wild idea: What if you could capture EVERY customer interaction, understand it with AI, and actually run your revenue org off of it? Back then: • Investors said: “sounds like a feature of CRM” • Analysts had no category • Customers asked: “is this Big Brother?” Fair questions. Today, Gong was named to Fast Company’s World’s Most Innovative Companies list (Applied AI) 🚀 Grateful to Fast Company for the recognition 🙏 But the real story isn’t the award. It’s that what used to feel crazy… is now growing crazy fast. Gong is one of the fastest-growing companies at our scale. Over 5,000 companies rely on Gong’s Revenue AI OS, including half of the Fortune 10 📈 We were never interested in building “another sales tool.” We set out to build an autonomous system: the Revenue AI OS. AI doesn’t matter if it just analyzes. It matters when it changes how work gets done. • automating the busywork • surfacing what actually matters • guiding decisions in real time That’s the gap between AI hype and AI that drives revenue. Grateful to the Gong team and our customers who made this real 🙌 Next decade? We’re going much bigger. To infinity and beyond! 😎 https://lnkd.in/emcFBkHu
title: 10 years ago, Eilon and I had this wild idea: What if you could capture EVERY customer interaction, understand it with AI, and actually run your revenue org off of it?

hook: Today, I’m excited to join TechArena.ai as a Co-Founding Advisor for TechArena Advisory.
text: Today, I’m excited to join TechArena.ai as a Co-Founding Advisor for TechArena Advisory. This new practice brings together 200+ years of collective experience across the technology industry—built by operators who have led through the very transformations companies are navigating today. I bring a distinctive combination of enterprise growth leadership, ecosystem strategy, and board governance—shaped by leading multi-billion-dollar businesses and scaling global partner ecosystems at critical inflection points, and reinforced through my service on the board of IDEX Corporation and as a board advisor to Responsive.io. As former Chief Technology Officer for Microsoft’s Global Partner Solutions organization, I operated at the center of one of the world’s largest partner ecosystems—driving multi-billion-dollar growth, scaling global routes-to-market, and enabling partners to monetize AI-powered solutions. Across Microsoft and Intel, I’ve consistently led transformations during periods of market and technology shift—aligning platform strategy, partner ecosystems, and go-to-market execution to unlock growth across cloud, AI, and data center environments. TechArena Advisory is a direct response to the existential challenges businesses face in navigating today’s accelerated technology landscape, as operators race to deploy trillions of dollars of capital equipment to fuel AI’s advancement. Traditional consultancy models cannot keep pace. Our practice spans the full growth lifecycle—from product development and market positioning to go-to-market execution and revenue acceleration. We help lay the foundation for ecosystem building, operational transformation, and financial governance to fuel growth and investor confidence. The Advisors aren’t prognosticating on the industry—we’ve helped build it. We operate at the intersection of strategy, execution, and growth—helping leaders translate AI ambition into enterprise-scale outcomes. If you're navigating AI transformation, ecosystem strategy, or growth at scale—I’d love to connect. Learn more: https://lnkd.in/gtSEccj9 Jeni Barovian Allyson Klein Laura St. John MBA Raejeanne Skillern Dana Bos
title: Today, I’m excited to join TechArena.ai as a Co-Founding Advisor for TechArena Advisory.

hook: Marketer says to founder: "Hey, there are 3 killer ways we could position this, but with our team, we only have the resources to pull off one.
text: Marketer says to founder: "Hey, there are 3 killer ways we could position this, but with our team, we only have the resources to pull off one. Which do you want: option 1, option 2, or option 3?" Founder: "Let's do all of 'em" Marketer: "Sorry, maybe I wasn't clear. We can't do all of them. We don't have enough reach to make any of them land. And even if we could, we'd be putting 3 different narratives into the market, confusing everyone." Founder: "We should do all of them." Marketer: "...did you just hear what I said?" Founder: "I just saw on LinkedIn that some guy built an omnichannel GTM program with one prompt and a bunch of agents." Marketer: "What was the name of that company? Do they make any money?" Founder: "I don't remember their name. Not sure about the revenue either." Marketer: "So we're changing our entire go-to-market strategy based on a LinkedIn post from a company you can't name that may or may not make money." Founder: "I'm going to send you the post. Actually, just ping my AI bot in Slack, he'll send you the link. I gotta run. Dinner with the investors." Marketer: "..." — And this is why most B2B positioning is a garbage. To the marketing leaders who have to put up with this shit, we salute you. 🫡
title: Marketer says to founder: "Hey, there are 3 killer ways we could position this, but with our team, we only have the resources to pull off one.

hook: In 2026, GTM teams will split into two groups: Teams chasing shiny AI tools Teams building REAL growth systems.
text: In 2026, GTM teams will split into two groups: Teams chasing shiny AI tools Teams building REAL growth systems. Guess which ones will win. The last 12 months were chaos for GTM teams. • New AI tools every week • New growth hacks every day • Endless “automations” everyone should try So teams panic. "We're not using this AI tool yet." "We should test this outbound workflow." "I heard this growth hack generates leads." Shiny object syndrome everywhere. But the teams that win in 2026 will focus on two things. 1. Strong GTM foundations Not sexy. But this drives revenue. • Clear ICP • Sharp positioning • Messaging that actually resonates • Offers built from real market research • A brand narrative that connects everything Most companies skip this part. Then wonder why their campaigns don’t work. 2. Systems that compound Not random tactics. Real GTM systems. • Intent signals • Warm outbound • Content repurposing engines • Personal brands for distribution • Sales + content alignment AI helps here. But AI is not the strategy. It’s just the workflow layer. The companies that win in 2026 will still test new tools. But they won’t lose focus. Because the real advantage isn't hacks. It’s clarity + systems. That’s also the thinking behind what we’re building at Content Path. Helping teams turn ideas into scalable content systems. Simple rule for 2026 GTM: Foundations → Systems → Tactics Not the other way around. If you're building GTM in 2026: Focus less on tactics. Focus more on infrastructure. What’s one GTM priority for 2026 you’d add?
title: In 2026, GTM teams will split into two groups: Teams chasing shiny AI tools Teams building REAL growth systems.

hook: Your 12-month roadmap is already obsolete.
text: Your 12-month roadmap is already obsolete. GoDaddy doesn't have one. They plan the next hypothesis instead. That line from leadership at GoDaddy changed how I think about roadmaps. Most companies plan 12 months out. GoDaddy plans the next hypothesis. Under CTO Charles Beadnall, they built Hivemind. An internal experimentation platform launched in 2020. Any engineer can propose a test. Data decides. Look at the scale: 2021: ~1,000 experiments 2022: 1,300+ 2023: 1,700+ 2024: 2,500+ Since 2019, A/B testing contributed to $1.6B in revenue growth. Win rates improved from under 5% to over 30% in two years. That is not luck. That is system design. Examples: Airo AI suite Six week AI surge. ~30 new features shipped. Early tests showed small businesses using Airo generated 28% more sales. Personalized AI domain search Generative AI recommendations drove 17.8% more domains purchased. WordPress targeting Identifying high propensity users led to 35% higher Managed WordPress conversions. They even showcase failed experiments internally. Top 10 tests. Wins, losses, draws. Failure is celebrated for the learning. The lesson for operators: Democratize experimentation Standardize rigor Use one north star metric Celebrate evidence over ego If you remove opinion from the room, progress speeds up. -- I study how companies turn experimentation into revenue. Follow for breakdowns you won't find anywhere else.
title: Your 12-month roadmap is already obsolete.

hook: Over the past few years at Canva, one thing has become very clear to me: Great operations is the ultimate force multiplier.
text: Over the past few years at Canva, one thing has become very clear to me: Great operations is the ultimate force multiplier. ✨ As our Marketing team has grown to 450+ people across Brand, Creative, Performance, International, MarTech, Research & Data, Product Marketing and more -- the difference between momentum and noise often comes down to how well we run. The right operator doesn’t just keep things moving. They make the whole system better. That’s why I’m hiring an Executive Operations Partner to join my core team, working directly alongside me and Ally Garrett, our Chief of Staff for Marketing. This role sits at the centre of our marketing engine and operates across two core domains: 1️⃣ CMO Operations Architecting how I operate to best deliver for Canva and the marketing team -- strategically and sustainably. Not just calendar management. Owning the full operating rhythm that drives clarity, pace and focus across Marketing. This includes: • Designing and running a high-leverage weekly & monthly cadence • Sequencing global priorities across time zones • Triage, trade-offs and proactive problem solving • Preparing briefs for leadership, board and external moments • Ensuring follow-through and accountability • Owning complex international travel & logistics • Reducing friction and protecting energy so I can focus on what matters most 2️⃣ Key Programs & Organisational Effectiveness Owning high-impact, cross-functional initiatives that shape how Marketing runs at scale. This includes: • Turning ambiguity into clear, trackable plans • Driving alignment across global teams • Raising risks early and proposing solutions • Building scalable workflows and systems • Powering our biggest moments — Cannes, Canva Create, Droptober, global offsites and major launches The goal: help our global marketing org operate with clarity, velocity and intent. I’ve seen how powerful this role can be. ➡️ Junia Ooi joined as one of our first operations hires — today she runs our LATAM business. ➡️ Ally Garrett scaled our global marketing operations and program management function — now leading a high powered ops group supporting our Consumer, B2B, Comms and Growth teams. ➡️ Veronica Tebbitt drove tremendous impact in a similar role and went on to support our CEO directly. This isn’t just a support role. It really is a launchpad. If you’re an exceptional operator who: • Thrives in complexity • Has strong judgement and isn’t afraid to push back • Gets energy from building systems that unlock teams • Is comfortable supporting C-suite leaders at pace • Already uses AI to work smarter • Has real ambition and gas in the tank I’d love to hear from you -- or from someone you rate highly!
title: Over the past few years at Canva, one thing has become very clear to me: Great operations is the ultimate force multiplier.

hook: Intercom was heading toward negative growth when the CEO came back after 2 years away.
text: Intercom was heading toward negative growth when the CEO came back after 2 years away. He spent $100M in cash, killed $60M ARR on purpose and moved 80% of R&D into Fin. Today: $400M ARR & doubled growth two years in a row. Three years ago, Intercom was heading toward negative growth. The CEO had stepped away for two years. When he came back, the company was dying quickly. So he systematically destroyed his own company. He replaced everything: • New values • New mission • New brand • New board • New pricing Moved 80% of R&D into a single AI product. Spent $100M in cash reserves when most people thought AI was hype. The result? Intercom doubled their growth rate two years in a row while every competitor in the space declined. They're now on track to be one of the fastest-growing large software companies in the world. They killed $60M in ARR on purpose. Then they went all in: • Shifted their entire customer base to a new pricing model • Reduced pricing on legacy products • Focused monetization on the AI agent Most founders would rather die than voluntarily destroy $60M in revenue. If you won't make these moves, someone else will: • Cannibalize your own revenue • Bet your cash reserves • Fire against new values to remove people who can't adapt Intercom committed corporate suicide and rebuilt from the mantra of All it will take is destroying everything you love. Most SaaS companies right now are doing the opposite: • Protecting what worked • Defending their margins • Adding AI features instead of becoming AI companies Meanwhile, the companies willing to burn it down are pulling away. Here's my takeaway as a bootstrapped founder: We don't have $100M in cash reserves to bet or 30,000 customers to migrate. But we have something Intercom didn't have three years ago. We have nothing left to protect. That's the advantage that matters right now.
title: Intercom was heading toward negative growth when the CEO came back after 2 years away.

hook: When we started Ground, I didn’t begin with a product.
text: When we started Ground, I didn’t begin with a product. I started with people. I reached out to the CEOs, Heads of Growth, and operators behind the brands I deeply respected - Brands like Equinox, Glossier, Netflix, NHL, to emerging ones like Adapt Naturals, and ROZ. the ones whose missions I believed in. I wanted to understand how they thought about growth, where they were constrained, and what they wished existed. That became Ground. Today, Ground is an autonomous AI revenue system of agents adding ~10% more topline revenue for the brands we work with - these agents operate continuously, inside their existing tools and systems, understanding buying signals and converting intent into revenue. But the part I didn’t expect: In the last 7 days, three customers - some current, some former (!) - reached back out… Not because of a campaign. Not because we chased them. Because they felt the absence and wanted more. One told me: “I have 40,000+ emails in my inbox, but I still remember yours from 2024. You actually understood what we were going through. You made me believe this was real before I fully did.” I told them to stop because I was on the verge of tears, haha. While we’ve always been building an AI company, what mattered early on wasn’t just the technology - it was how we showed up. We educated. We listened. We cared enough to be early - and more importantly, to be patient (even though I’m still working on this one). Inside Ground, I always ask the team to keep a “wish list.” Which companies do you want to see win? Which operators deserve to get promoted? Whose mission actually matters? Because if you build with that level of intentionality, the product compounds in a different way. Today, Ground has driven millions in revenue for brands - often after a ~15 minute onboarding. But what’s more important is this: We’re not just creating artificial intelligence or training agents. We’re building the system that helps the right businesses reach their potential.
title: When we started Ground, I didn’t begin with a product.

hook: It's finally time to introduce Kerrigan Robotics to the world!
text: It's finally time to introduce Kerrigan Robotics to the world! After a year of keeping things under wraps while we developed, we're super excited and ready for our first public announcement. I'm thrilled to announce that Sanjay Lal is joining as Co-Founder & CEO, while I step into the Co-Founder & CTO role. This is the right leader at exactly the right moment, and a milestone that reflects the incredible scale we have reached based on the work our team has put in, including engineers like Christian Cassar and Bill Rose, who've been with us from the start. As I continue to look at manufacturing, there is still a massive Physical AI Deployment Gap. It’s trapped in "pilot purgatory" because most systems can't orchestrate and reason across the grit, heterogeneous complexity, and 24/7 pressure of a real production line. We are building the agentic AI nervous system for manufacturing - a unified layer that manages the entire automation lifecycle from design to 24/7 operations. Built on the pedigree of orchestrating thousands of robots at Tesla, we are now bringing that same industrial intelligence to our customers in the semiconductor, F&B, and automotive sectors - and we’re just getting started. I am honored to co-lead this team of automation veterans, and I’m grateful for the partnership of our investors at Transition Ventures (David Helgason & Mona Alsubaei), Overture Ventures (Shomik Dutta, Brandon Hurlbut & Leila Pirbay), Foothill Ventures (Eric Rosenblum & Theresa (Wei) Dai), and WovenEarth Ventures (Jane Woodward) as we scale this vision. The future of manufacturing isn't just about more robots - it’s about Industrial Reasoning.
title: It's finally time to introduce Kerrigan Robotics to the world!

hook: Tomorrow is a very important day for Product School, and I keep thinking about something Robby Stein said on our podcast: "As a founder, as a leader, everyone needs to think, what's gonna be the most important thing that if we get it right, this is potentially a five or 10 year opportunity of growth, of value, of helpfulness that we can give people." Tomorrow, we're launching something big, something that – we hope – can help raise a new generation of AI-native product managers.
text: Tomorrow is a very important day for Product School, and I keep thinking about something Robby Stein said on our podcast: "As a founder, as a leader, everyone needs to think, what's gonna be the most important thing that if we get it right, this is potentially a five or 10 year opportunity of growth, of value, of helpfulness that we can give people." Tomorrow, we're launching something big, something that – we hope – can help raise a new generation of AI-native product managers. The role of a PM now comes with a lot of new expectations. We have been tirelessly releasing brand-new AI-first courses as well as updating our original certifications to account for the new skills necessary. With this new launch, we want to make learning these crucial new skills more accessible – so the industry transformation goes faster and smoother. More details tomorrow
